pretest.question: Which fraction is the greatest: 3/8, 6/8, or 2/8?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.5 'Compare and Order Fractions' pp.369-372
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions

learn.title: 같은 분모 정렬 — 분자만 보기
learn.content: To ORDER fractions, compare them two at a time using the strategies you know. Step 1: Find the smallest (or largest) fraction. Step 2: Compare the remaining fractions. Step 3: Write them in order. Example: Order 4/6, 1/6, 5/6 from least to greatest. All have denominator 6 → just order numerators: 1 < 4 < 5 → 1/6, 4/6, 5/6. 예: Order from least to greatest: 7/8, 2/8, 4/8 → 2/8, 4/8, 7/8. (Same denominator (8). Order numerators: 2 < 4 < 7.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.5 'Compare and Order Fractions' pp.369-372
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 같은 분자 정렬 — 분모 큰 순
learn.content: When fractions have the SAME NUMERATOR, remember: smaller denominator = bigger pieces = greater fraction. Example: Order 3/8, 3/4, 3/6 from greatest to least. Same numerator (3). Order denominators smallest to largest for greatest to least: 4, 6, 8 → 3/4, 3/6, 3/8. 예: Order from greatest to least: 1/3, 1/8, 1/6 → 1/3, 1/6, 1/8. (Same numerator (1). Smallest denominator = greatest fraction. 3 < 6 < 8 → 1/3 > 1/6 > 1/8.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.5 'Compare and Order Fractions' pp.369-372
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 벤치마크로 분류 후 정렬
learn.content: When fractions have DIFFERENT numerators AND denominators, use the 1/2 benchmark to sort them into groups first. Group A: less than 1/2. Group B: equal to 1/2. Group C: greater than 1/2. Then compare within each group. Example: Order 1/8, 5/6, 4/8. → 1/8 < 1/2 (Group A). 4/8 = 1/2 (Group B). 5/6 > 1/2 (Group C). → Least to greatest: 1/8, 4/8, 5/6. 예: Sort into groups: 7/8, 2/6, 3/6. Which is less than 1/2? Equal to 1/2? Greater than 1/2? → 2/6 < 1/2, 3/6 = 1/2, 7/8 > 1/2. (2/6: 2 < 3 (half of 6) → less than 1/2. 3/6: 3 = 3 → equal to 1/2. 7/8: 7 > 4 (half of 8) → greater than 1/2.)

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.5 'Compare and Order Fractions' pp.369-372
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 정렬 3단계 루틴
learn.content: 여러 분수 정렬 3단계:   단계 1) 모든 분수를 한 줄에 적기.   단계 2) 공통 패턴 찾기 (같은 분모 / 같은 분자 / 다 다름).   단계 3) 패턴별 규칙 적용 → 작은 → 큰 순으로 다시 적기. 예: 5/8, 1/8, 7/8 → 같은 분모 → 분자 1, 5, 7 → 1/8 < 5/8 < 7/8.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.5 'Compare and Order Fractions' pp.369-372
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 두 분수 사이 — 'between'
learn.content: 두 분수 사이의 분수 찾기:   단계 1) 양 끝 값 확인 (예: 2/8과 6/8).   단계 2) 같은 분모로 표현 가능한지 확인.   단계 3) 사이 값 선택. 예: 2/8과 6/8 사이 → 3/8, 4/8, 5/8 모두 가능. 🔍 수직선 위에서 순서가 시각적으로 잘 보임.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.5 'Compare and Order Fractions' pp.369-372
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 정렬 검산 — 인접 쌍 비교
learn.content: 정렬한 결과를 검산: 인접한 두 분수씩 짝지어 부등호 확인.   예: 1/8 < 3/8 < 1/2 < 5/6 →     1/8 < 3/8 ✓, 3/8 < 1/2 ✓, 1/2 < 5/6 ✓. 🔍 모든 인접 쌍이 통과하면 전체 순서 정확. 한 곳이라도 틀리면 그 부분을 다시 정렬.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.5 'Compare and Order Fractions' pp.369-372
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 실생활 정렬 — 결승선/물 채우기
learn.content: 실생활 문제에서 정렬:   '같은 거리 4명 달리기 → 7/8, 5/6, 1/2, 3/8 완주' →     완주 적은 순 = 분수 작은 순 → 3/8 < 1/2 < 5/6 < 7/8.   '같은 크기 항아리 4개 채움' 등도 동일 원리. 🔍 핵심 가정: '같은 전체' (같은 거리, 같은 항아리).

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.5 'Compare and Order Fractions' pp.369-372
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r2.question: Three jars are the same size. Jar A is 3/4 full. Jar B is 3/8 full. Jar C is 3/6 full. Order the jars from most full to least full.
practice_r2.answer: Jar A (3/4), Jar C (3/6), Jar B (3/8)

practice_r3.question: Kai says to order 1/3, 1/6, 1/8 from least to greatest, you just order the denominators from least to greatest: 1/3, 1/6, 1/8. Is Kai correct? Explain.
practice_r3.answer: Kai is NOT correct. With the same numerator, a LARGER denominator means SMALLER pieces. So 1/8 < 1/6 < 1/3. The correct order from least to greatest is 1/8, 1/6, 1/3 — the denominators go from greatest to least.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Lesson 9.5 'Compare and Order Fractions' pp.369-372
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic F — Comparison, Order, and Size of Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

practice_r3.question: Kai says: 'To order 1/3, 1/6, 1/8 from least to greatest, just look at denominators in order.' Why is that wrong?
choices: A. 같은 분자에서는 분모 큰 순 = 작은 분수 순 → 1/8 < 1/6 < 1/3
practice_r3.explanation: 같은 분자라면 분모 큰 분수가 작음.
